findings: Multi-model rotation is implemented only at the launcher level via --models (comma-separated) and assigned round-robin per worker/commander (not per task). See bin/stampede.sh rotation parse + modulo assignment.

Metaswarm premium model policy is expressed as metadata (fleet.json: model_policy/premium_model_pool/banned_child_models) and as an instruction in the commander prompt to rotate a premium pool for sub-agents; the shell launcher itself only sets the commander pane model (from --models/--model) and does not enforce sub-agent model rotation beyond the prompt.

Silent fallback risk: if a custom --agent-cmd template does not include the {model} placeholder, the per-worker rotation is effectively ignored (model not passed), allowing the underlying CLI/tool to silently use its own default model.

Silent fallback / mis-detection risk: preflight probes only a single model (first of MODEL_LIST or MODEL), and the later 'Model availability' check message keys off $MODEL (even when --models is used), so invalid/unavailable models later in the rotation list may not be detected before launch, and the displayed model in the error may be misleading.

Premium-default mismatch risk: launcher default model is claude-haiku-4.5, while the orchestrator skill documents default model claude-sonnet-4.5; if the skill/run invocation fails to pass --model explicitly, runs may silently downgrade to a cheaper/non-premium default.
